text:
Once upon a time, in a village where every tree spoke its own language, there lived a girl named Lily. She loved adventure more than anything — climbing the oldest oak, sailing across the quiet lake, or just sitting by the stream listening to the crickets.

One morning, Lily woke up feeling particularly magical. She decided to visit Mr. Whispering Willow, the oldest tree. As she edged closer, he began to giggle. "Ah, Lily! I've had a thousand tales told beneath my branches!"

She pounced onto a branch to listen better. "What's your latest?"

"A fox once said he'd brought me the seeds for my thousandth catnip blossom!"

"Lucky me!" she giggled.

She listened long and hard until he stopped.

"Ah, my favorite story? Once upon a time…"

"Lilies always bloom?"

"No, larks. Each one sings a different song. One sang of adventure, one of sea voyages..."
